source_jobs.title: Electronics Systems Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ELECTRONICS SYSTEMS ENGINEER 💡


The Role:

We’re looking for a hands-on Electronics Engineer who loves solving complex problems and bringing ambitious technology to life. You’ll be central to designing, prototyping, and optimising advanced electronics for cold atom and quantum systems.

This isn’t a “follow the manual” job, you’ll help shape your role, work across disciplines, and tackle real engineering challenges head-on. If you thrive in a start-up environment, adaptable, innovative, and ready to persevere, this role is for you.


What You'll Do:

 - Collaborate with scientists & engineers to define system requirements for quantum technologies.

 - Design, develop & test low-noise analog and digital circuits.

 - Implement control and data acquisition systems for cutting-edge experiments.

 - Debug & optimise complex electronic hardware for reliable performance.

 - Integrate and test next-generation quantum devices.

 - Continuously improve hardware & software solutions.



What We Are Looking For:

 - BSc/MSc in Electrical/Electronics Engineering (PhD or Systems Engineering background is a plus).

 - 3+ years’ experience in electronics design and prototyping for high-performance systems.

 - Strong skills in analog & digital circuit design, PCB layout, and prototyping.

 - Experience with GHz RF circuits highly desirable.

 - Background in cold atom, FPGA, or quantum technologies (atomic clocks, sensors, computing) is a big advantage.

 - Proficiency with relevant programming languages & design tools.

 - Excellent problem-solving and communication skills.

 - Start-up mindset: proactive, flexible, and hands-on


What We Offer:

💰 Salary: £40k + Bonuses
🎯 Milestone bonuses: 2x per year (June & December, ~£3.4k each)
📈 Share options
💡 Enhanced pension scheme
🌍 Overseas travel opportunities


Benefits:

 - 26 days annual leave + company closure over Christmas/New Year

 - Flexible hours (10am-4pm core hours)

 - Regular team outings, training & knowledge-sharing sessions (every 2 weeks)

 - Annual performance & salary review
   

⚛️ About Us:

At Aquark Technologies, we’re on a mission to reshape the future of sensing and quantum computing. Spun out from the University of Southampton, we’re building the world’s most portable, high-performance miniaturised cold atom traps, pioneering devices that will unlock new possibilities in quantum technology.

Following a major research contract win, we’re expanding our team. This is your chance to join a fast-moving start-up, work on cutting-edge science and engineering, and make a tangible impact.

source_jobs.title: Talent Pool
source_jobs.description:
🚀 JOIN THE AQUARK TALENT POOL ⚛️


📍 Location: Eastleigh, Southampton – 5 days a week on site
💼 Type: Talent Pool – Future Opportunities



🌟 ABOUT US

At Aquark, we’re on a mission to bring quantum technology out of the lab and into the real world. Born as a spin-out from the University of Southampton, we’re building the world’s most compact cold atom core, enabling the next generation of quantum sensing and computing to power breakthroughs across industries.

Following a successful ignition funding round, we’re now laser-focused on creating a low-power, robust, real-world-ready cold atom trap and we want curious, ambitious people like you to be part of this journey.



💡 LIFE AT AQUARK

Aquark HQ is a hubbub of science, collaboration, and good banter. Our team is passionate, bold, and right on the bleeding edge of quantum tech. We work closely together, bounce ideas off each other daily, and believe great breakthroughs happen when smart people share coffee and conversations in the same space.

We’re flexibly on site 5 days a week in our Eastleigh facility, building, testing, and pushing boundaries side by side (10am - 4pm as core hours). 📍



🔭 OUR TEAMS


We’re growing across several departments and always keen to meet exceptional talent in:

 - 🧪 Science – cold atom physics, quantum sensing, experimentation

 - ⚙️ Hardware & Firmware Engineering – building the systems that make quantum tech real

 - 💻 Software Engineering – control systems, automation, and data pipelines

 - 📈 Commercial – partnerships, strategy, and taking our tech to market

 - 🌍 +More...


⭐️👉 Even if we don’t have a live vacancy right now, we’d love to hear from you. By joining our Talent Pool, you’ll be among the first we contact as soon as new opportunities arise.



🎁 WHAT WE OFFER

Benefits:

 - 26 days annual leave + company closure over Christmas/New Year

 - Flexible working hours (10am–4pm core hours)

 - Regular team outings, training & knowledge-sharing sessions (every 2 weeks)

 - Annual performance & salary reviews 🎯

 - Competitive salary 💰

 - Milestone bonuses

 - Share options 📈

 - Enhanced pension scheme

 - Overseas travel opportunities 🌍
   
   


🚀 JOIN US

If you’re excited by the idea of helping shape the future of quantum technology , whether you’re a scientist, engineer, software developer, commercial trailblazer or just super curious, we’d love to hear from you.



👉 Submit your CV and a short note about why you’re interested in Aquark Technologies.

Even if the perfect role isn’t available today, we’ll be in touch when it is.


🔗 Download our Whitepaper: Hardware for Educating Quantum Engineers https://www.aquarktechnologies.com/technology
📚 Follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/aquark-technologies/?originalSubdomain=uk

source_jobs.title: Vacuum Materials Scientist
source_jobs.description:
Description:

Aquark Technologies is a start-up with a vision to create disruptive technology in sensing and quantum computing by delivering the world’s most portable, scalable and high performing miniaturised cold atom systems.

As a Vacuum Materials Scientist, you will directly develop the technology used to improve vacuum performance that underpins our quantum sensor technology. You will research, test, and define materials, coatings, and getter strategies, and design and interpret experiments to ensure our systems reliably achieve and maintain ultra-high vacuum over extended timescales. The understanding and development of stable alkali vapour systems, including achieving long-term control of vapour pressure and compatibility within sealed environments, will be key.

This role sits within a research programme (funded by UKRI FLF) to transform traditional vacuum hardware into compact, robust, and scalable platforms, enabling a shift from bespoke laboratory setups to manufacturable, off-the-shelf solutions. Working at the intersection of vacuum science, materials engineering, and microfabrication within a fast-paced startup environment, you will collaborate with a driven team to deliver innovative, high-impact technologies while contributing to the growth of a dynamic and evolving company.

 

Responsibilities:

 - To improve vacuum quality and lifetime, including investigating appropriate materials, getters, gas permeation barriers, long-term sealing methods, and vacuum stability.

 - Develop alkali vapour systems, including ensuring stable and controllable rubidium operation under UHV conditions, and vapour cell environments.

 - Develop and apply modelling and simulation tools to predict vacuum performance, as needed.

 - Provide technical direction for experimental collaboration with other team members.

 - Carry out production coatings using established, optimised processes to deliver consistent and repeatable results, as needed.

 

Qualifications:

Essential

· PhD (or equivalent experience) in Physics, Materials Science, or a related field

 - Strong expertise in vacuum-compatible materials, surface interactions, ultra-high vacuum (UHV) systems and vacuum physics (outgassing, permeation, leak detection).

 

Desirable

 - Knowledge of thin-film getter coatings

 - Experience with alkali vapour systems (e.g. rubidium) and sealed environments

 - Proven ability to design experiments and analyse data to guide technical decisions.

 - Ability to work independently, problem-solve, demonstrate initiative, and engage in continuous learning to adopt new skills.

 - Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines in a fast-paced R&D environment

 

Renumeration:

 - Total salary and bonus up to: £46,000 to £72,000 (salary dependent on experience)
   
   - Base salary: £39,428.57 to £61,714.29
   
   - Company Milestone Bonus Scheme, up to: £6,571.43 to £10,285.71

(2x £3,285.71 to £5,142.86 payments).

 - Full earnings pension contribution:
   
   - Employer contribution – 3%
   
   - Employee contribution – 4%
   
   - Relief at source – 1%

 

Other Benefits:

 - Private Medical Insurance .

 - Group Life Insurance .

 - Employee Assistance Programme.

 - 26 Days of holiday + Public holidays.

 - Flexibility around core hours.

 - Regular company outings, team building and training opportunities.

 - Electric Vehicle Salary Sacrifice.

 - Encouraged learning to continue professional development.

source_jobs.title: Vacuum Process Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Description:

Aquark Technologies is a start-up with a vision to create disruptive technology in sensing and quantum computing by delivering the world’s most portable, scalable and high performing miniaturised cold atom systems.

As a Vacuum Process Engineer, you will be responsible for translating vacuum concepts into robust, repeatable, and scalable fabrication processes for miniaturised vacuum systems and miniaturised vapour cells. You will design and implement bonding, sealing, and assembly techniques, ensuring they are reproducible and suitable for future manufacturing.

This role sits within a research programme (funded by UKRI FLF) to transform traditional vacuum hardware into compact, robust, and scalable platforms, enabling a shift from bespoke laboratory setups to manufacturable, off-the-shelf solutions. Working at the intersection of vacuum science, materials engineering, and microfabrication within a fast-paced startup environment, you will collaborate with a driven team to deliver innovative, high-impact technologies while contributing to the growth of a dynamic and evolving company.

Responsibilities:

 - Develop miniaturised vapour and UHV cells using microfabrication techniques, including integration of alkali sources and hermetic sealing processes.

 - Fabricate chambers using MEMS techniques, including testing innovations with active pumping and passive pumping.

 - Support, as needed, the modelling of sealing and fabrication processes, ensuring simulation inputs reflect realistic geometries, materials, and manufacturing constraints.

 - Train other staff as needed.



Qualifications:

Essential

 - Degree (BSc/MSc/PhD or equivalent experience) in Engineering, Physics, Materials Science, or a related field

 - Experience with vacuum system design, assembly, and operation (high vacuum or UHV)

 - Strong mechanical aptitude with hands-on experience in building, assembling, or prototyping mechanical systems.

 - Strong understanding of vacuum-compatible materials and sealing techniques



Desirable

 - Experience with microfabrication or precision manufacturing processes (e.g. bonding, MEMS, wafer-level techniques)

 - Knowledge of hermetic sealing methods (e.g. anodic bonding, thermocompression, brazing, laser welding)

 - Proficiency in data analysis, modelling, or automation desired.

 - Familiarity with alkali vapour cells

 - Ability to work independently, problem-solve, demonstrate initiative, and engage in continuous learning to adopt new skills.

 - Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines in a fast-paced R&D environment



Renumeration:

 - Total salary and bonus up to: £46,000 to £72,000 (salary dependent on experience)
   
   - Base salary: £39,428.57 to £61,714.29
   
   - Company Milestone Bonus Scheme, up to: £6,571.43 to £10,285.71 (2x £3,285.71 to £5,142.86 payments).

 - Full earnings pension contribution:
   
   - Employer contribution – 3%
   
   - Employee contribution – 4%
   
   - Relief at source – 1%



Other Benefits:

 - Private Medical Insurance.

 - Group Life Insurance.

 - Employee Assistance Programme.

 - 26 Days of holiday + Public holidays.

 - Flexibility around core hours.

 - Regular company outings, team building and training opportunities.

 - Electric Vehicle Salary Sacrifice.

 - Encouraged learning to continue professional development.

notes: Quantum technology startup with a focus on engineering and scientific roles.
